summary: Cleaned up HSD_JObj flag naming and usage across baselib, item, ground, and lb code. The PR prefixed JObj flag macros with JOBJ_, added helper masks/predicates such as JOBJ_ROOT_MASK and union_type_spline, replaced raw bit constants with named flags, and collapsed large hand-expanded inline JObj transform setters in item.c back into calls like HSD_JObjSetRotationY, HSD_JObjSetTranslate, and HSD_JObjSetScale. It closed issue #536. Review evidence is limited to two approvals with no written comments.

key_files.role: Renamed JObj flag macros to JOBJ_* names, added JOBJ_ROOT_MASK, added/updated union_type_* predicates, and replaced raw flag checks in inline setters.
Propagated JOBJ_* flag names through JObj traversal, animation, loading, display, parent transparency-bit recalculation, and accessor logic.
Removed large manually expanded JObj setter/assert/dirty-marking sequences and replaced them with HSD_JObjSetRotationY, HSD_JObjSetTranslate, and HSD_JObjSetScale calls.
Replaced raw/old JObj flags with JOBJ_INSTANCE, JOBJ_MTX_INDEP_SRT, JOBJ_MTX_DIRTY, and JOBJ_USE_QUATERNION.
Replaced raw 0x1000 instance flag check with JOBJ_INSTANCE.
Replaced raw 0x1000 instance flag check with JOBJ_INSTANCE.

what_changed: Renamed bare JObj flag macros such as SKELETON, HIDDEN, MTX_DIRTY, INSTANCE, MTX_INDEP_SRT, ROOT_OPA, ROOT_XLU, and ROOT_TEXEDGE to JOBJ_SKELETON, JOBJ_HIDDEN, JOBJ_MTX_DIRTY, JOBJ_INSTANCE, JOBJ_MTX_INDEP_SRT, JOBJ_ROOT_OPA, JOBJ_ROOT_XLU, and JOBJ_ROOT_TEXEDGE.
Removed the old function-like JOBJ_INSTANCE(o) macro and used JOBJ_INSTANCE as a flag constant, making condition polarity explicit at call sites such as JObjLoadJointSub and HSD_JObjResolveRefs.
Added JOBJ_ROOT_MASK as the OR of JOBJ_ROOT_OPA, JOBJ_ROOT_TEXEDGE, and JOBJ_ROOT_XLU and used it in transparency/root-bit recalculation instead of raw 0x70000000-style masks.
Updated union-type helpers: union_type_ptcl now checks JOBJ_PTCL, union_type_spline was added for JOBJ_SPLINE, and union_type_dobj now returns false for either PTCL or SPLINE.
Replaced raw flag constants such as 0x1000 and 0x02000000 with JOBJ_INSTANCE and JOBJ_MTX_INDEP_SRT in ground/gr/lb/item-related code.
In item.c, replaced repeated hand-expanded inline setter logic, including asserts and HSD_JObjSetMtxDirtySub paths, with calls to HSD_JObjSetRotationY, HSD_JObjSetTranslate, and HSD_JObjSetScale.
Simplified item facing-direction code to call HSD_JObjSetRotationY(item_jobj2, (M_PI / 2) * item_data->x2C_facing_direction).
Updated the decomp.me scratch link comment for func_80268E5C from In9qm to cSrfX.
smart_moves: Centralized JObj transform mutations through the existing inline setters instead of duplicating assert and dirty-matrix behavior in item.c, cutting a large amount of decompiled boilerplate while preserving the intended HSD_JObjSetMtxDirty path.
Prefixed all generic flag names with JOBJ_ to avoid global macro collisions and make bit checks self-documenting in non-baselib files.
Converted magic numbers to named flags in small caller files first, making traversal and instance-skip logic easier to audit.
Kept matching-sensitive behavior visible by changing condition polarity explicitly when replacing the old inverted JOBJ_INSTANCE(o) macro with a plain JOBJ_INSTANCE flag constant.
Added union_type_spline and folded spline handling into union_type_dobj, documenting that DObj payload selection excludes both PTCL and SPLINE cases.
decomp_lessons: When an inline helper expands into many asserts, direct field writes, and dirty-flag logic, replacing the expansion with the named inline call can drastically simplify a function and often improve readability without losing matching intent.
Avoid unscoped macro names in shared headers; broad names like HIDDEN, INSTANCE, and MTX_DIRTY should be system-prefixed once their ownership is known.
Be careful when replacing a predicate macro with a flag constant: old macros may have inverted semantics, so each call site must be checked rather than mechanically renamed.
Named masks such as JOBJ_ROOT_MASK are preferable to repeated composed constants because they make bit propagation code easier to review and search.
Union-discriminating macros should express all known excluded variants; here DObj detection was updated to exclude both particle and spline JObjs.
naming_conventions: Use JOBJ_* for HSD_JObj flag bits: JOBJ_SKELETON, JOBJ_HIDDEN, JOBJ_INSTANCE, JOBJ_MTX_DIRTY, JOBJ_MTX_INDEP_SRT, JOBJ_USE_QUATERNION, and related root/union flags.
Use JOBJ_ROOT_MASK for combined root transparency/render flags instead of hand-written ORs or raw constants.
Keep HSD_JObj* API helper names for mutating transforms, e.g. HSD_JObjSetRotationY, HSD_JObjSetTranslate, HSD_JObjSetScale.
Use union_type_dobj, union_type_ptcl, and union_type_spline for JObj union payload checks rather than ad hoc flag tests.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use inline HSD_JObj setter wrappers to reproduce the compiler's expected assert/field-store/dirty-matrix pattern while avoiding manually written duplicated code.
Replace raw flag constants only after confirming the exact bit value, e.g. 0x1000 corresponds to JOBJ_INSTANCE and 0x02000000 corresponds to JOBJ_MTX_INDEP_SRT.
For matching code that had expanded inline logic, introducing small inline wrapper calls can remove temporary variables such as phi flags and temp flag copies created by decompilation.
Explicit boolean expressions like `(itemStateFlags & ITEM_ANIM_UPDATE)` and `(jobj->flags & JOBJ_INSTANCE)` were used instead of `!= FALSE` or magic-number checks where the diff shows simplification.

review_feedback.note: No issue comments or review comments were present in the PR slice, so there is no written reviewer rationale to extract.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that continue replacing raw JObj flag constants with JOBJ_* names.
Search for remaining bare macros or raw constants equivalent to JOBJ_INSTANCE, JOBJ_MTX_DIRTY, or JOBJ_MTX_INDEP_SRT.
Audit uses of union_type_dobj to ensure spline JObjs are consistently excluded after this PR.
Look for other item.c functions with manually expanded HSD_JObjSetTranslate, HSD_JObjSetScale, or HSD_JObjSetRotation* logic that can be collapsed.
Inspect issue #536 for the original motivation behind the JObj cleanup if more context is needed.
